pick.rationale: Michigan State's 3-seed and Tom Izzo's legendary March track record give the Spartans a meaningful edge in this matchup, particularly given Louisville's lingering uncertainty around star point guard Mikel Brown Jr.'s back injury—Louisville went just 7-5 without him during the regular season. While Louisville's advanced metrics (KenPom #18, Torvik #16, AdjO 124.1) are arguably superior to Michigan State's raw efficiency numbers (ORtg 117.3, DRtg 101.7), the Spartans' elite schedule strength (8th nationally, SRS 23.44) and Big Ten battle-testing provide a more reliable floor, and their 25-7 record against that gauntlet reflects a team built for tournament-style physicality. The health disparity and Izzo's proven ability to maximize his roster in March tip this close matchup toward Michigan State, though a fully healthy Louisville would make this essentially a coin flip.

reasoningStep.title: Louisville vs Michigan State – East Round of 32
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan State's higher seed, elite schedule strength, and Tom Izzo's March pedigree give them the edge over a Louisville team dealing with Mikel Brown Jr.'s uncertain health status.
evidence: Louisville: KenPom #18 (AdjEM +25.42, AdjO 124.1, AdjD 98.7), Torvik #16, 23-10 ACC
Mikel Brown Jr. questionable with back injury; Louisville went 7-5 without him this season
Michigan State: AP #8, 25-7 (15-5 Big Ten), SRS 23.44 (10th), SOS 12.91 (8th), ORtg 117.3 (49th), DRtg 101.7 (58th)
Michigan State KenPom ~#13 mid-season; Big Ten schedule provides superior battle-testing
Louisville's raw efficiency metrics are stronger but Brown Jr.'s injury narrows the gap significantly
Tom Izzo's extensive March Madness pedigree favors Michigan State in close tournament matchups

Set Bracket Now! Is Mikel Brown Jr. Playing? Latest on Louisville Star’s Injury Status for March Madness Tournament Opener Men's CBB Is Mikel Brown Jr. Playing? Latest on Louisville Star’s Injury Status for March Madness Tournament Opener Mikel Brown Jr. is questionable for Louisville's NCAA Tournament opener as Pat Kelsey reports encouraging progress on the star's back. March 17, 2026 | 3:05 PM EDT Mikel Brown Jr. hasn’t played a basketball game in nearly three weeks. Louisville’s star freshman guard, a projected lottery pick who tied Wes Unseld’s program scoring record with 45 points against NC State last month, has been reduced to watching his teammates practice while nursing the back injury that’s defined his first college season. Now, with the Cardinals facing No. 11 seed South Florida on Thursday in the NCAA Tournament’s first round, Brown’s status remains officially questionable. Louisville coach Pat Kelsey offered an encouraging update Monday, telling reporters, “He made great progress last week. We’ll see how the next couple days of practice go.” The Cardinals (23-10) need Brown in the lineup if they’re going to make a deep run. result.text: Spartans sliding in NCAA NET, KenPom rankings following recent losing skid Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Women's Basketball Hockey Tom Izzo Recruiting Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports MSU basketball sliding in NCAA NET, KenPom rankings amid recent losing skid Robert Bondy Spartans Wire It has been a rough stretch of games for the Spartans recently, which has resulted in them starting to slip in two of the most notable rating systems in college basketball. Michigan State has lost three of their last four games, with the most recent defeat coming at Wisconsin in blowout fashion. The Spartans trailed essentially the entire game en route to a 20-plus point loss to the Badgers. With the loss to Wisconsin, Michigan State is now 20-5 overall and 10-4 in Big Ten play. The Spartans are still looking like a lock to reach the NCAA Tournament this year, but how has the recent slump impacted their ratings in KenPom and NET? Well, they've started to slide in both. Through games on Saturday, Michigan State sits at No. 13 in the KenPom rankings . For NET, Michigan State is a few spots lower at No. 15 in those rankings . Michigan State will look to get things back on track next week with a pair of home games ahead. The Spartans will first play UCLA on Tuesday night before Ohio State comes to town on Sunday. The Spartans could really use wins in both of those games to help bolster their NCAA Tournament resume and start moving back up the rankings. Contact/Follow us @ The SpartansWire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Michigan State news, notes and opinion. You can also follow Robert Bondy on X @ RobertBondy5 . Latest on Louisville Star’s Injury Status for March Madness Tournament Opener Mikel Brown Jr. is questionable for Louisville's NCAA Tournament opener as Pat Kelsey reports encouraging progress on the star's back. March 17, 2026 | 3:05 PM EDT Mikel Brown Jr. hasn’t played a basketball game in nearly three weeks. Louisville’s star freshman guard, a projected lottery pick who tied Wes Unseld’s program scoring record with 45 points against NC State last month, has been reduced to watching his teammates practice while nursing the back injury that’s defined his first college season. Now, with the Cardinals facing No. 11 seed South Florida on Thursday in the NCAA Tournament’s first round, Brown’s status remains officially questionable. Louisville coach Pat Kelsey offered an encouraging update Monday, telling reporters, “He made great progress last week. We’ll see how the next couple days of practice go.” The Cardinals (23-10) need Brown in the lineup if they’re going to make a deep run. result.text: Spartans sliding in NCAA NET, KenPom rankings following recent losing skid Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Women's Basketball Hockey Tom Izzo Recruiting Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports MSU basketball sliding in NCAA NET, KenPom rankings amid recent losing skid Robert Bondy Spartans Wire It has been a rough stretch of games for the Spartans recently, which has resulted in them starting to slip in two of the most notable rating systems in college basketball. Michigan State has lost three of their last four games, with the most recent defeat coming at Wisconsin in blowout fashion. The Spartans trailed essentially the entire game en route to a 20-plus point loss to the Badgers. With the loss to Wisconsin, Michigan State is now 20-5 overall and 10-4 in Big Ten play. The Spartans are still looking like a lock to reach the NCAA Tournament this year, but how has the recent slump impacted their ratings in KenPom and NET? Well, they've started to slide in both. Through games on Saturday, Michigan State sits at No. 13 in the KenPom rankings . For NET, Michigan State is a few spots lower at No. 15 in those rankings . Michigan State will look to get things back on track next week with a pair of home games ahead. The Spartans will first play UCLA on Tuesday night before Ohio State comes to town on Sunday. The Spartans could really use wins in both of those games to help bolster their NCAA Tournament resume and start moving back up the rankings. Contact/Follow us @ The SpartansWire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Michigan State news, notes and opinion. You can also follow Robert Bondy on X @ RobertBondy5 . - Louisville's advanced metrics (KenPom #18, Torvik #16) are actually quite strong - potentially better than Michigan State's KenPom ranking (~#13 mid-season but with a late-season slide)
- Louisville has a significantly better offense (AdjO 124.1 vs MSU's 117.3) and comparable or better defense (AdjD 98.7 vs MSU's 101.7)

- The Mikel Brown Jr. injury is the key wildcard - if he doesn't play, Louisville is significantly weakened
- Michigan State is the higher seed (3 vs 6) and has Tom Izzo's tournament experience

This is actually closer than the seeding suggests. Louisville's efficiency metrics are arguably better, but the Brown Jr. injury and Michigan State's tougher schedule/higher seed create uncertainty. Michigan State as a 3-seed with Izzo in March and Louisville's health concerns tip this toward MSU.
